title: head writer of Eva Q
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] head writer of Eva Q             Quote Post by peripateia » Sun Apr 28, 2013 5:22 pm Does anybody know who wrote the script and dialogue for 3.0? Was Anno chiefly responsible for these changes, or were there other writers who altered the scenario/plot? I ask because it seems to be a radical departure from previous Rebuild films' characterizations. The general impression I've gotten from Eva Q is that the characters seem to resemble their 2.0 counterparts, but without the time taken from NGE or EoE to flesh out their intricacies, 3.0 versions of Shinji, Rei, Gendo, Misato (at least to me) rely largely on the work of previous films in molding their personalities. I can't make sense of these new characters with the same names-and suspect Anno may not be the only person to have influenced the plot. Apologies if there is already a thread for this, if anybody familiar with the credits of Eva 3.33 could tell me anything about the people involved with the film's writing, feel free to reply!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 28, 2013 5:30 pm Anno will have been in overall control, but IIRC the credited circle of writers has changed for 3.0 compared with previous installments.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sun Apr 28, 2013 5:32 pm The best that I can gather from how these films are written is that some combination of Anno, Tsurumaki, Enokido, Yamaga, Higuchi, Masayuki, or any other friends get together and bat around story ideas. Anno takes all the ideas and begins writing drafts.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 28, 2013 5:34 pm Anno receives sole credit for planning and screenplay.

[]:             Quote Post by peripateia » Tue Apr 30, 2013 11:04 pm I just have this feeling that the people who wrote NGE+EoE introduced a set of unique characters with quirks, traumas and demons, whereas the person/people who wrote Eva 2.0 and 3.0 worked from NGE and EoE without really contributing to these personalities (and simplifying them at times). I must say that if Anno had written both EoE and Eva 3.0, the latter is a sore disappointment for me. If some other writers contributed, then I'd rather blame the new guy. Having said that, I still appreciate Rebuild-as fan service, high-quality animation, and nostalgia. I liked Anno's work better when he was a depressed wreck!

oE introduced a set of unique characters with quirks, traumas and demons, whereas the person/people who wrote Eva 2.0 and 3.0 worked from NGE and EoE without really contributing to these personalities (and simplifying them at times).
